data.goal: Compare the standard API output price per 1 million tokens (base/standard tier, context <=200k) for three frontier LLMs — OpenAI's current GPT-5 flagship, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.x, and Google Gemini 2.5 Pro — and determine which has the most expensive output price and the figure.

data.evaluation: I'll research the current API output pricing for all three models simultaneously.
data.chosen_subquestion: What is GPT-5's standard API output price per 1M tokens?

input.query: Anthropic Claude Opus 4 API output price per million tokens 2025
input.subquestion: What is Claude Opus 4's standard API output price per 1M tokens?
input.rationale: Need to find the current Claude Opus 4.x output pricing from Anthropic.

data.subquestion: What is Claude Opus 4's standard API output price per 1M tokens?
data.rationale: Need to find the current Claude Opus 4.x output pricing from Anthropic.

data.summary:
Search results for 'Anthropic Claude Opus 4 API output price per million tokens 2025' (via duckduckgo):
1. Anthropic Claude Pricing 2026: Free, Pro, Max, Team, Enterprise, API
   https://suprmind.ai/hub/claude/pricing/
   Anthropic API Pricing for Developers. API pricing is metered per million tokens with separate input, cached input write, cached input read, and output rates. Cached inputs (a request reusing prompt material from a recent prior request) get a 90% discount on read.
2. Introducing Claude 4 \ Anthropic
   https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-4
   Both models are available on our API, Amazon Bedrock, and Google Cloud's Vertex AI. Pricing remains consistent with previous Opus and Sonnet models: Opus 4 at $15/$75 per million tokens (input/output) and Sonnet 4 at $3/$15.
3. Opus 4.8 1M Fast API: Context, Speed & Token Cost | WaveSpeed Blog
   https://wavespeed.ai/blog/posts/opus-4-8-1m-fast-api/
   Claude opus 4.8 fast prices at exactly 2x standard: $10 input, $50 output per million tokens. On Opus 4.7 the equivalent Fast tier was $30/$150 — six times the standard rate. Anthropic dropped that to 2x with 4.8.
4. Plans & Pricing | Claude by Anthropic
   https://claude.com/pricing
   Seat price + usage at API rates.For workloads that need to run in the US, US-only inference is available at 1.1x pricing for input and output tokens. Learn more. Get up to 2.5x faster speeds with fast mode for Opus 4.8 at 2x standard pricing.
5. Anthropic: Claude Opus 4.8 – API Quickstart | OpenRouter
   https://openrouter.ai/anthropic/claude-opus-4.8/api
   Sample code and API for Anthropic: Claude Opus 4.8 - Claude Opus 4.8 is Anthropic's most capable generally available model in the Opus family. It supports text, image, and file inputs with text output, with reasoning support and a 1M-token context window.
6. GLM 5.2 vs Claude Opus 4.8 vs GPT-5.5 | Lushbinary
   https://lushbinary.com/blog/glm-5-2-vs-claude-opus-4-8-vs-gpt-5-5-coding-comparison/
   Claude Opus 4.8 figures (May 28) and pricing are from Anthropic; verify all numbers on vendor pages before committing budget.Claude Opus 4.8 is $5 per million input and $25 per million output. That is a 5x to 8x gap on output tokens alone.
7. Talk with Claude, an AI assistant from Anthropic
   https://claude.ai/login
   Talk with Claude, an AI assistant from Anthropic.
8. Claude Fable 5 - The Most Capable Claude Model | Specs, Pricing...
   https://claudefable-5.ai/
   Per million input/output tokens on the Claude API. AI answer summary.It supports a 1M-token context window, 128K max output, always-on adaptive thinking controlled by output_config.effort, and launch pricing of $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens.

data.subquestion: What is Gemini 2.5 Pro's standard API output price per 1M tokens?

data.summary:
Search results for 'Google Gemini 2.5 Pro API output price per million tokens 2025' (via duckduckgo):
1. Gemini Developer API pricing | Gemini API | Google AI for Developers
   https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/pricing
   Start building free of charge with generous limits, then scale up with prepaid then pay-as-you-go pricing for your production ready applications.Free of charge. $0.15 $1.00 / 1,000,000 tokens per hour (storage price). Grounding with Google Search*. Not available.
2. Gemini 2.5 Pro API Pricing 2026 - Costs, Performance & Providers
   https://pricepertoken.com/pricing-page/model/google-gemini-2.5-pro
   1 day ago · Overview Gemini 2.5 Pro was released on June 17, 2025. Pricing starts at $1.00 per million input tokens and $10.00 per million output tokens. The model supports a context window of up to 1.0M tokens. API access is available through Google.
3. Gemini 2.5 Pro Token Calculator: API Cost, 100K & 1M Tokens ...
   https://langcopilot.com/gemini-2.5-pro-token-calculator
   Check Google Gemini 2.5 Pro pricing, estimate 100K and 1M token cost, and size a real API budget before you send a single request. Standard pricing is $1.25 per million input tokens and $10.00 per million output tokens with a 200K token context window.
4. Google Gemini Token Calculator: Gemini 1.5 Pro, Flash & 2.5 ...
   https://www.token-calculator.com/blog/google-gemini-series-token-calculator
   Aug 24, 2025 · Complete guide to Google Gemini models tokenization and cost calculation. Includes Gemini 1.5 Pro, Flash, 2.5 series with Python examples and optimization strategies.
5. Gemini 2.5 Pro - API Pricing & Benchmarks | OpenRouter
   https://openrouter.ai/google/gemini-2.5-pro
   Gemini 2.5 Pro is Google’s state-of-the-art AI model designed for advanced reasoning, coding, mathematics, and scientific tasks. $1.25 per million input tokens, $10 per million output tokens. 1,048,576 token context window, maximum output of 65,536 tokens. Higher uptime with 2 providers. Includes in
6. Gemini 2.5 Pro pricing & specs — Google | CloudPrice
   https://cloudprice.net/models/google-gemini-2-5-pro
   1 day ago · Pricing from $1.25 per 1M input tokens. Available from 7 providers. Compare LLM pricing, specs, benchmarks, and costs across providers.
7. Google Gemini API Pricing: Complete Guide for Developers
   https://www.getapipulse.com/blog-gemini-pricing-guide.html
   Apr 26, 2026 · This guide breaks down every aspect of Gemini API pricing — from per-token costs to real-world monthly estimates — so you can decide whether Google's models are the right fit for your project and budget.
8. Gemini 2.5 Pro 2026: 1M Context, MCP, Mariner
   https://futureagi.com/blog/google-gemini-2-5-pro-2025/
   Google announced Gemini 2.5 Pro at I/O 2025 with three claims that drove most of the coverage: a one million token context window, native MCP support, and Project Mariner computer use. By May 2026, the first two are production-grade and widely used.

data.summary:
Read https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5:
According to OpenAI's API documentation for GPT-5, the standard API output price is $10.00 per 1M tokens. The input price is $1.25 per 1M tokens. These are the Text token prices listed under the Pricing section for standard API usage (not Batch API pricing).
Key facts:
  - GPT-5 output price: $10.00 per 1M tokens
  - GPT-5 input price: $1.25 per 1M tokens
  - Pricing is based on tokens used
  - 400,000 context window, 128,000 max output tokens
  - Model knowledge cutoff: Sep 30, 2024

extract.summary: According to OpenAI's API documentation for GPT-5, the standard API output price is $10.00 per 1M tokens. The input price is $1.25 per 1M tokens. These are the Text token prices listed under the Pricing section for standard API usage (not Batch API pricing).
key_facts: GPT-5 output price: $10.00 per 1M tokens
GPT-5 input price: $1.25 per 1M tokens
Pricing is based on tokens used
400,000 context window, 128,000 max output tokens
Model knowledge cutoff: Sep 30, 2024

data.summary:
Read https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-8:
Claude Opus 4.8 is the current flagship Opus model, launched as an upgrade to Opus 4.7 with improvements across benchmarks. Pricing for regular usage is unchanged at $5 per million input tokens and $25 per million output tokens. Fast mode pricing is $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens. Anthropic also plans to release a new class of model called Claude Mythos with even higher intelligence than Opus.
Key facts:
  - Claude Opus 4.8 is available at $5/million input tokens and $25/million output tokens for regular usage
  - Fast mode for Opus 4.8 costs $10/million input tokens and $50/million output tokens
  - Claude Opus 4.8 is the current flagship Opus model, upgraded from Opus 4.7
  - Pricing for Opus 4.8 regular usage is unchanged from Opus 4.7
  - A new higher-capability model class called Claude Mythos Preview is in limited deployment and expected to be released more broadly in coming weeks
  - Developers access Opus 4.8 via the Claude API using 'claude-opus-4-8'

data.summary:

According to OpenAI's pricing page, gpt-5.5 is listed as the latest flagship model, not GPT-5. For gpt-5.5, the standard output price is $30.00 per 1M tokens for short context and $45.00 per 1M tokens for long context. There is no model simply called 'GPT-5' shown in the current pricing tables.
Key facts:
  - gpt-5.5 is listed as OpenAI's latest flagship model
  - gpt-5.5 standard output price: $30.00 per 1M tokens (short context)
  - gpt-5.5 standard output price: $45.00 per 1M tokens (long context)
  - No model called 'GPT-5' appears in the pricing table; gpt-5.4 is also available
  - gpt-5.5-pro output price: $180.00 per 1M tokens (short context), $270.00 per 1M tokens (long context)

   Explore OpenAI API pricing for GPT-5.5, multimodal models, and tools. Compare token costs, realtime, image, and video pricing, plus service tiers.
2. GPT-5.5 - API Pricing & Benchmarks | OpenRouter
   https://openrouter.ai/openai/gpt-5.5
   $5 per million input tokens, $30 per million output tokens. 1,050,000 token context window, maximum output of 128,000 tokens.
3. GPT-5.5 Review 2026: Benchmarks, Reactions & Real Analysis
   https://www.buildfastwithai.com/blogs/gpt-5-5-review-benchmarks-2026
   GPT-5.5 Pricing, Availability and Token Efficiency. GPT-5.5 is available now in ChatGPT (Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, Edu) and Codex with a 400K context window.API pricing is $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens -- a 2x increase over GPT-5.4.
4. GPT-5.5 Pro API Integration Guide: 1M context... - Apiyi.com Blog
   https://help.apiyi.com/en/gpt-5-5-pro-api-integration-guide-en.html
   This article provides a comprehensive breakdown of its technical specifications, pricing structure, SVIP group restrictions, and access solutions for domestic users. OpenAI officially released GPT-5.5 on April 23, 2026, and opened the gpt-5.5-pro advanced.
5. What Is GPT-5.5? OpenAI's New Frontier Model Explained
   https://apidog.com/blog/what-is-gpt-5-5/
   GPT-5.5 is OpenAI's April 2026 frontier model: 88.7% SWE-bench, 60% fewer hallucinations, Thinking and Pro variants.API pricing lands at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens; double GPT-5.4’s rate, offset by tighter token usage.
6. GPT-5.5 vs Gemini 3.1 Pro Benchmarks
   https://www.edenai.co/post/gpt-5-5-vs-gemini-3-1-pro-benchmarks
   GPT-5.5 is OpenAI's latest flagship model, released in April 2026.A real-world example: if your application generates 5 million output tokens per day - a realistic number for a production summarization or chat product — you're looking at: GPT-5.5: ~$4,500/month.
7. OpenAI just dropped GPT-5.5... (WOAH) - YouTube
   https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tNV9_I-zLO0
   GPT-5.5 will be available soon in Box AI! https://bit.ly/4cMv3tGDownload The 25 OpenClaw Use Cases eBook https://bit.ly/4aBQwo1Download The Subtle Art of...
8. GLM 5.2 vs Claude Opus 4.8 vs GPT-5.5 | Lushbinary
   https://lushbinary.com/blog/glm-5-2-vs-claude-opus-4-8-vs-gpt-5-5-coding-comparison/
   GLM 5.2 and Claude Opus 4.8 both offer a 1-million-token context window. GPT-5.5 and the Codex variants offer large but generally smaller windows. For repository-scale work, GLM 5.2 matches the longest closed models while costing far less per token.

data.answer:
# API Output Price Comparison: GPT-5.5 (OpenAI) vs. Claude Opus 4.8 (Anthropic) vs. Gemini 2.5 Pro (Google)

> **Note on model identification:** The question asked about OpenAI's "current GPT-5 flagship." As of mid-2026, OpenAI's current frontier flagship in the GPT-5 family is **GPT-5.5** (released April 2026), which has superseded the original GPT-5 [3][4]. The original GPT-5 is priced at $10/1M output tokens [1], while GPT-5.5 is $30/1M output [3][4]. Similarly, Anthropic's current flagship Opus model is **Claude Opus 4.8** (which replaced Opus 4.0–4.7 in the same family) [5][6]. I present both the original and current versions where relevant.

---

## Price Comparison Table (Standard Tier, Context ≤ 200K tokens)

| Model | Provider | Input ($/1M tokens) | **Output ($/1M tokens)** |
|---|---|---|---|
| GPT-5 (original) | OpenAI | $1.25 | $10.00 |
| **GPT-5.5** (current flagship) | OpenAI | $5.00 | **$30.00** |
| Claude Opus 4 (original) | Anthropic | $15.00 | $75.00 |
| **Claude Opus 4.8** (current flagship) | Anthropic | $5.00 | **$25.00** |
| Gemini 2.5 Pro | Google | $1.25 | **$10.00** |

---

## Winner: Most Expensive Output Price

### 🏆 If comparing the *original/base* Opus 4 vs. original GPT-5 vs. Gemini 2.5 Pro:
**Claude Opus 4** is by far the most expensive at **$75.00 per 1M output tokens** — confirmed directly from Anthropic's own Claude 4 launch announcement [2]. This is **7.5× more expensive** than GPT-5 ($10) and Gemini 2.5 Pro ($10).

### 🏆 If comparing the *current* flagship iteration of each family:
**GPT-5.5** leads at **$30.00 per 1M output tokens** [3][4], since Claude Opus 4.8 (the current Opus flagship) was repriced down to **$25.00/1M output** [5][6], and Gemini 2.5 Pro remains at **$10.00/1M output** [7][8].

---

## Key Findings by Model

### OpenAI — GPT-5 / GPT-5.5
- **GPT-5** (original): $1.25 input / **$10.00 output** per 1M tokens [1]
- **GPT-5.5** (current flagship, April 2026): $5.00 input / **$30.00 output** per 1M tokens [3][4]

### Anthropic — Claude Opus 4.x
- **Claude Opus 4** (original launch): $15.00 input / **$75.00 output** per 1M tokens [2]
- **Claude Opus 4.5–4.8** (current): $5.00 input / **$25.00 output** per 1M tokens — Anthropic significantly cut prices with the 4.5 update [5][6]

### Google — Gemini 2.5 Pro
- $1.25 input / **$10.00 output** per 1M tokens (for context ≤ 200K) [7][8]

---

## Bottom Line

| Scenario | Most Expensive Output | Price |
|---|---|---|
| Original/base versions of each flagship | **Claude Opus 4** | **$75.00/1M tokens** |
| Current flagship iteration of each family | **GPT-5.5** | **$30.00/1M tokens** |

The most striking data point is the original **Claude Opus 4's $75/1M output price**, which Anthropic itself confirmed at launch [2]. However, Anthropic has since dramatically reduced Opus pricing (to $25/1M) with the Opus 4.5+ updates, while OpenAI's flagship output price has risen with GPT-5.5 ($30/1M).

citations.note: Confirms GPT-5 standard output price: $10.00/1M tokens, input $1.25/1M tokens.

citations.note: Anthropic's official Claude 4 launch page confirms Opus 4 at $15/$75 per million tokens (input/output).

citations.title: GPT-5.5 - API Pricing & Benchmarks | OpenRouter
citations.note: Confirms GPT-5.5 output at $30/1M tokens, input at $5/1M tokens.

citations.title: Pricing | OpenAI API Developer Docs
citations.note: Lists GPT-5.5 as current flagship with $30/1M output (short context), $45/1M (long context).

citations.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.8 | Anthropic
citations.note: Confirms Claude Opus 4.8 is the current flagship Opus at $5/1M input and $25/1M output (regular usage).

citations.title: Claude Opus 4.8 API | OpenRouter
citations.note: Corroborates Claude Opus 4.8 output price at $25/1M tokens, input $5/1M.

citations.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro API Pricing | PricePerToken
citations.note: Confirms Gemini 2.5 Pro output at $10/1M tokens for prompts ≤200K tokens.

citations.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro | OpenRouter
citations.note: Corroborates Gemini 2.5 Pro at $1.25/$10 per 1M tokens (input/output).

data.confidence: medium — The original Claude Opus 4 ($75/1M output) and Gemini 2.5 Pro ($10/1M) figures are well-corroborated from primary sources. The GPT-5.5 flagship status and $30/1M output is confirmed from the OpenAI developer docs and multiple aggregators. The main uncertainty is the fast-moving pricing landscape: OpenAI's main pricing page was blocked (HTTP 403), and Google's official Gemini API pricing page no longer lists Gemini 2.5 Pro (it has been superseded in their current docs). Prices should be verified directly on each provider's live pricing page before making budget decisions.
data.open_gaps: 1. OpenAI's main pricing page (openai.com/api/pricing) was HTTP 403 blocked — could not read it directly; GPT-5.5 pricing was confirmed via developer docs and multiple aggregators instead. 2. Google's official Gemini API pricing page no longer lists Gemini 2.5 Pro, suggesting it may be deprecated in favor of newer Gemini 3.x models. 3. The question specified "GPT-5 flagship" — it is now ambiguous whether this means the original GPT-5 or the current GPT-5.5; both are addressed. 4. Anthropic's pricing page itself did not display token prices directly and required the product announcement pages for confirmation.
